title: alaternus sempervirens var. latifolia
intro: Alaternus sempervirens var. latifolia, also known as the Italian buckthorn or broad-leaved alder, is a plant species that belongs to the family Rhamnaceae and is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antiseptic, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin and wounds. The plant is also valued for its health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and acting as a diuretic. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the Italian buckthorn can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a medical professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
